Ad Text:
TechnologyOne Specialists | Analysts | Project Managers

We’re partnering with organisations across local government, utilities, education, healthcare, and the not-for-profit sector who are investing heavily in ERP and broader digital transformation programs. These organisations are modernising core systems, improving service delivery, and scaling capability across TechnologyOne and other enterprise platforms.

They’re looking for experienced professionals who can support the delivery of complex transformation initiatives across business and technology environments.

The Role

These roles sit at the centre of business, technology, and transformation delivery, supporting organisations as they implement, enhance, and optimise enterprise systems such as TechnologyOne and other ERP platforms.

You’ll work across business stakeholders, technical teams, and delivery functions to ensure systems, processes, and data are aligned to organisational goals and operational needs.

Key Responsibilities

Support the delivery of ERP and digital transformation programs across TechnologyOne and similar platforms

Gather, analyse, and translate business requirements into functional and technical specifications

Work across data migration, system integration, and platform enhancement activities

Partner with stakeholders to improve business processes and system outcomes

Contribute to the delivery of reporting, insights, and data-driven decision-making

Collaborate with cross-functional teams across IT, business, and vendors

Support Agile or hybrid delivery environments using tools such as JIRA and Confluence

Document system changes, processes, and solution designs to support ongoing improvement

What We’re Looking For

Experience in Business Analysis, Project Delivery, or ERP implementation environments

Strong understanding of TechnologyOne or similar ERP systems (SAP, Oracle, Dynamics 365, highly regarded)

Ability to work across business and technical stakeholders in complex environments

Experience supporting or delivering digital transformation or system uplift programs

Strong communication, documentation, and stakeholder engagement skills

Delivery-focused mindset with the ability to work in evolving program environments

Ideal Background

We’re particularly interested in candidates from:

Local government, utilities, education, healthcare, or not-for-profit sectors

ERP transformation or enterprise system implementation programs

Consulting or systems integrator environments supporting ERP delivery

Organisations modernising legacy systems and moving toward cloud-based platforms

Why These Roles

These roles sit within organisations undergoing significant transformation, where ERP systems and digital capability are critical to improving operational efficiency and service delivery. You’ll be part of programs that directly impact how organisations run, scale, and evolve their core systems.

If you’re open to contract opportunities within ERP and transformation environments, we’d love to hear from you.

Ventia, Evolve Program
Title - Senior Business Analyst
Location - North Sydney Office, Hybrid Working 3 days per week in office

Primary Responsibilities

The Evolve Program, is a key priority of Ventia's multi-year strategic transformation program to modernise its ERP, HR/Payroll, data and integration platforms to upgrade the legacy SAP ECC 6 environment to S4Hanna, SuccessFactors and EC Pay. A key work stream within this transformation is the selection and implementation of a best of breed Rostering, Timesheeting and Attendance that will integrate into the overall strategic platform architecture.

This role will provide end-to-end requirements ownership and leadership across the selection, design, and implementation of a Rostering and Time & Attendance (RTA) solution for a large, geographically dispersed field services workforce, integrated with SAP Payroll and SAP SuccessFactors.

The role owns the business requirements, and will be a key participantant in vendor evaluation, solution fit assessment, and payroll compliance outcomes. The Senior BA ensures the selected solution is operationally fit, payroll accurate, scalable, and aligned to SAP design principles.

Experience and Qualifications

Essential:

Demonstrated experience leading the requirements capture and definition for RTA solution implementations.
Strong Experience with SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central, SAP Payroll Integation and HCM Time Management & Rostering Solutions (SAP or other)
Strong understanding of; Time wage types and schema rules, Payroll interfaces and posting logic, Award interpretation and compliance scenarios
Strong stakeholder engagement and communication skills
Proven experience with RFP and vendor selection processes for enterprise systems
Strong experience in field services, utilities, maintenance, or mobile workforce environments
Deep understanding of; Time capture, Shift scheduling, Payroll compliance, allowances, overtime, and other work schedule exceptions and adjustments.
Strong documentation, facilitation, and decision support skills

Desirable:

Experience with RTA solution implementations that support 10,000+ exployees and over 30 collective agreements.
SAP Certification(s) in HCM or SuccessFactors and 5+ years of experience in an SAP environment
Exposure to third-party Workforce Management solutions (e.g. Kronos/UKG)
Experience in large enterprise or heavily unionised environments
Knowledge of Australian, NZ, or EMEA industrial relations frameworks
SAP Payroll time evaluation and wage type configuration knowledge
SuccessFactors Employee Central exposure
Experience in unionised environments
BA certifications (CBAP, CCBA, Agile BA).

General Responsibilities

RFP, Vendor Selection & Commercial Support (Senior Accountability):

Lead the business analysis workstream for T&A RFP and vendor selection activities
Own the development of:

1.Business, functional, non functional, integration, and compliance requirements
2.Field based use cases and critical payroll scenarios

Design and facilitate vendor demonstrations and proof of concept scenarios, focused on:
Field services complexity
Roster Scheduling
Attendance, clocking in/out
Award and interpratino for Payroll
Lead functional evaluation of vendor responses and demos, identifying:
Gaps, workarounds, and delivery risks
SAP integration constraints and dependencies
Provide clear, evidence based recommendations to procurement, architecture forums, and steering committees
Support contract clarification and implementation scoping discussions post selection

Business Analysis & Operational Design:

Own end to end requirements definition for rostering and time capture, including:
Mobile and remote workforce models
Travel time, start/finish locations, and site-based work
On-call, call-outs, standby, and emergency response
Overtime, penalties, allowances, and fatigue management
Lead functional solution design across the T&A platform and SAP landscape
Partner with HCM & Payroll Functional Leads to:
Design time evaluation, wage type mapping, and payroll schemas
Define inbound and outbound integrations for employee, organisational, and payroll data
Validate end to end functional design from; Rostering → time capture → approvals → SAP Payroll results
Ensuring payroll and industrial compliance outcomes
Interpret Awards, EBAs, and employment contracts relevant to field services roles
Ensure the solution supports; Minimum breaks and maximum working hours, Overtime, penalty, and allowance calculations, Fatigue, safety, and regulatory constraints.
Identify risks early and escalate with mitigation options and recommendations

Test Planning and Execution:

Partner with the Program Test Lead to validate;
The testing strategy
SIT and UAT scope
Parallel payroll testing approach
Test scenarios and acceptance criteria for complex field and payroll scenarios
Payroll validation and defect triage
Ensure traceability from requirement → solution configuration → test outcome → payroll result.

Change, Deployment and Adoption:

Provide input into change and adoption strategy
Support readiness assessments and cutover planning
Act as senior business support during go live and payroll stabilisation periods

Key Experiences & Skills

Lead role in solicitation and capture of requirements for a RTA solution.
Experience in award interpretation and translation to functional requirements.
Exposure to enterprise grade, rostering, timesheeting and attendance solutions.
Experience with SAP SuccessFactors (Employee Central, EC Time)
Exposure to SAP HCM (Time, Payroll)
Comfortable operating across business, technical, and vendor teams
Able to simplify and interpret complex requirements and enterprise agreements into clear and efficient functional requirements.

Key working relationships

RTA Project Manager (Direct Line)
HCM Solution Architect
RTA Functional Lead
Organisational Change Lead
Integration Technology Manager
Evolve Data Migration and Reporting Leads
System Integrator Technical Leads
PMO, Functional, Testing, Change & Communications teams
Business SME's & Operations Leaders & Schedulers
Payroll Leadership (SAP Payroll)
HR Leadership (SAP SuccessFactors)
Industrial Relations & Legal

Job Title: Data, Reporting & Insights Specialist - Enterprise Risk
Ad Text:
This is a great opportunity for an experienced data analytics and reporting professional to drive, uplift and enhance enterprise risk reporting and insights capability within this leading industry superfund.

It is a newly created role that reports to the Senior Enterprise Risk Partner and will be responsible for delivering enhanced, accurate and informative reporting & insights to internal and external stakeholders to support operational and strategic decision making. 

The role will partner with senior leaders enabling confident governance, stronger risk foresight, and enhanced strategic outcomes through high-quality data and reporting. 

Key responsibilities include:

Lead enterprise-wide risk reporting and insights, shaping how risk information is interpreted, contextualised, and presented to key stakeholders including Executive, Board and Committees.
Design, uplift and embed fit-for-purpose reporting aligned to Fund's Risk Management Framework, governance obligations, and regulatory expectations.
Deliver Board-quality reporting–clear, concise, and insightful–supporting effective oversight and informed decision-making.
Extract, analyse and interpret data from the GRC system, turning complex datasets into meaningful insights and emerging risk.
Partner with senior stakeholders across the business, engaging in trusted, high-impact conversations to influence outcomes and drive risk maturity.
Provide forward-looking insights, identifying trends, emerging risks and opportunities using analytics, scenario analysis and evolving tools (including AI).
Champion consistency, accountability and continuous improvement in enterprise risk reporting practices.
Continually evolve reporting capability, enhancing automation, visualisation, and storytelling.
This is a thought leadership and influencing role, requiring judgement, credibility, and the ability to engage at senior and executive level with confidence.

Candidate Experience

Extensive experience in a senior enterprise risk, data, reporting or insights leadership role, ideally within superannuation or financial services.
Demonstrated experience delivering Executive and Board-level reporting, with confidence presenting and engaging at Board/Committee level.
Strong executive presence and gravitas – able to influence, challenge constructively, and build trust with senior leaders and governance bodies.
Expertise in enterprise risk frameworks, regulatory environments (APRA, ASIC), and trend-based risk analysis preferred.
Hands-on experience with GRC platforms (e.g. Archer, Protecht) and the automation of risk reporting processes.
Advanced capability in data extraction, transformation, visualisation and storytelling, using tools such as Microsoft Power BI, Excel and PowerPoint.
Experience evolving reporting frameworks, templates and methodologies with a forward-looking, strategic lens.
Relevant tertiary qualifications in commerce, finance, risk, data analytics, data science or a related discipline.

The Reach Agency is the leading digital development agency specializing in creating secure and compliant web solutions. With over 15 years of experience and more than 500 successful web projects, we have partnered with some of the world's largest banking, finance, and legal firms.

Role Overview

You are responsible for turning client business requirements into clear, deliverable software solutions.

Acting as the bridge between client and delivery teams, you develop a deep understanding of the client's business, processes and constraints, and define solutions that are practical, scalable and aligned with Reach's platforms.

You own solution definition end-to-end -- from discovery through to delivery -- ensuring what is designed, built and delivered remains aligned to client expectations and contractual scope.

You represent the client internally while maintaining commercial and technical discipline -- ensuring solutions are feasible, in scope and delivered to a high standard.

Reach is implementing an AI-first development model, and this role will play a key part in shaping how AI enhances solution design and delivery - expanding the positive impact and long-term relevance of this position




Key Responsibilities

Own the Client Context

Develop deep understanding of client business models and represent the client internally.

Lead discovery, workshops and requirements elicitation across business and technical stakeholders

Translate client problems into clear, prioritised and actionable solution requirements

Ensure internal decisions reflect real client needs and constraints

Own the Solution

Own end-to-end solution design across business, functional and system levels.

Define solution design across business, functional and system levels

Produce high-quality artefacts -- functional specs, workflows, use cases, system and user journeys

Ensure solutions align to Reach architecture, constraints, AI-enabled best practice and emerging delivery patterns

Provide clear, implementable specifications to engineering teams

Guide integration design, data flows and operational processes

Own Delivery Alignment

Align internal teams to deliver the defined solution to quality standards.

Align internal teams to the defined solution and ensure consistent execution

Ensure requirements are accurately represented in delivery tools (e.g. JIRA)

Support estimation and structuring of delivery workstreams

Identify and manage delivery risks and solution gaps early

Ensure what is built matches defined requirements and design

Own Quality and Validation

Define acceptance criteria and guide QA strategy and validation.

Define acceptance criteria and success conditions for all solution components

Guide QA strategy, test cases and validation approach

Ensure testing validates business behaviour, not just technical completion

Support UAT and ensure successful client acceptance

Drive low-defect, right-first-time delivery

Own Data and SaaS Implementation

Oversee SaaS delivery and data processes, ensuring operational readiness and alignment.

Oversee data mapping, migration and validation

Ensure solutions handle data correctly across systems and workflows

Lead SaaS implementation readiness -- configuration, integration and operational fit

Ensure solutions are scalable, reliable and maintainable

Own Scope and Commercial Integrity

Own scope integrity, managing commercial boundaries and change.

Maintain clear understanding of contracted scope and commercial boundaries

Identify out-of-scope requests early and drive appropriate change processes

Support estimation and scoping of new requirements

Ensure delivery remains aligned to agreed commercial outcomes

Own the Client Relationship

Act as primary interface between client and internal teams.

Maintain strong, direct engagement with client stakeholders

Ensure alignment between client expectations and delivery outcomes

Communicate solution decisions, trade-offs and progress clearly

Skills and Experience

Required Experience

Significant experience in Business Analysis, Solution Design or Technical Consulting roles

Proven experience working with external clients in software delivery environments

Strong understanding of software development lifecycle (SDLC)

Experience delivering web applications, SaaS platforms or enterprise systems

Experience working in Agile or hybrid delivery environments

Technical and Analytical Skills

Strong capability in requirements elicitation and functional specification

Experience designing workflows, system behaviours and solution structures

Familiarity with web technologies, APIs and data models

Experience working with relational databases and data processes

Experience using delivery tools such as JIRA

Professional Skills

Strong analytical and problem-solving capability

Clear and structured communication, both written and verbal

Ability to translate complex technical concepts for non-technical stakeholders

Strong stakeholder management and client engagement skills

Ability to manage multiple workstreams and priorities

Personal Attributes

Strong ownership and accountability for outcomes

Commercial awareness and client-focused mindset

Ability to balance business needs, technical constraints and delivery realities

Collaborative approach across engineering, QA and client teams

Attention to detail with the ability to think strategically

Openness to evolving delivery models, including the integration of AI into solution design and delivery practices




What can we offer you?

· You'll be working at the pointy end of an exciting industry.

· You'll be directly engaging with very smart, very professional and very motivated colleagues.

· You'll have ability to make changes and make things happen.

· City location, close to transport, with some WFH opportunities.

· Growth and career opportunities.

· Salary packaging · Flexible working conditions

· Opportunity to participate in a profit share scheme

About the job:

The Assessment Lead provides strategic and operational leadership for AI‑enabled assessment across CPA Australia’s professional development and professional programs. Reporting to the Head of Education Strategy, the role drives the implementation of a future‑focused assessment strategy, ensuring assessments are authentic, robust, compliant, and defensible. It oversees assessment design, quality assurance, and academic integrity, while embedding ethical and transparent use of AI technologies. The role also supports assessment operations, governance, and stakeholder engagement, using data and insights to continuously improve assessment practices and deliver high‑quality outcomes for learners and the profession.

This is an initial 12 month fixed term contract.




About you:

You are a strategically minded assessment professional with advanced accounting expertise and experience in higher or professional education. You bring strong capability in authentic assessment design, quality assurance, moderation, and academic integrity. You have hands‑on experience using AI‑enabled assessment technologies, including marking, analytics, and integrity tools, with a clear understanding of ethical and governance requirements. You are organised, detail‑focused, and confident managing assessment operations in live environments. You build strong stakeholder relationships, translate sector insights into practice, and use data to drive continuous improvement.




Key responsibilities:

Develop and maintain authentic, robust, and future focused assessment solutions for L&I products.

Contribute and oversea the design and rollout of new assessment methodologies, policies, frameworks, rubrics, and quality assurance mechanisms

Drive alignment with global best practices, supporting continuous improvement and innovation.

Oversea and support assessors and moderators with the use of AI marking tools, ensuring clarity on system outputs, limitations, and escalation pathways.

Act as a frontline operational contact for assessor and moderators on technical issues during assessment windows.

Develop, implement, and maintain robust assessment policies, governance structures, and assurance frameworks.

Oversee and implement strategies that safeguard assessment authenticity, including the use of structured assessment design to reduce risks of plagiarism, collusion, contract cheating, and misuse of generative AI.

Build strong relationships with academics, SMEs, employers, assessors, moderators, and vendors.

Provide insights, reporting, and recommendations to enhance effectiveness, candidate experience, and educational impact.

Category Finance Manager

Opportunity to grow your career with Kraft Heinz! 

Flexible work arrangement – 3 days in office, 2 days remote 

Collaborative and dynamic work environment 




The Kraft Heinz Company is one of the largest food and beverage companies in the world, with eight $1 billion+ brands and global sales of approximately $25 billion. Our brands are truly global, with products produced and marketed in over 40 countries. These beloved products include condiments and sauces, cheese and dairy, meals, meats, refreshment beverages, coffee, infant and nutrition products, and numerous other grocery products in a portfolio of more than 200 legacy and emerging brands.




Unlock Your Potential as a Category Finance Manager

The Category Finance Manager is the commercial business partner and P&L owner for the ANZ Taste Elevation cluster—one of our largest and most strategically important portfolios, including Heinz Ketchup, Heinz Mayonnaise, and the Gravox brand.

In this role, you will partner with the Head of Taste Elevation and help shape the portfolio by owning the business cases for the innovation, renovation, and value engineering. You will also drive performance through monthly reporting and forecast reviews, translating insights into action in partnership with Marketing, Category, Revenue Management, and Sales. In addition, you will support building the annual operating plan and three-year plan, ensuring a strong pipeline of initiatives to grow the Taste Elevation portfolio.




Key responsibilities

Own cluster performance: by conducting variance analysis on the YTD P&L, deriving insights and prescribing corrective measures

Lead forecast: review changes to the cluster forecast cycle on cycle and challenge where required, manage cluster risks and opportunities and participate in the relevant Integrated Business Planning (IBP) forums

Support the Plan: provide key inputs into the annual operating plan including owning cluster initiatives from the innovation pipeline and marketing programs

Commercial decision support: evaluate and challenge pricing, trade spend, and customer opportunities to optimise ROI and payback.

Own business cases: partner with Marketing on innovation, renovation and value engineering projects and track post-launch performance.

Executive reporting and storytelling: deliver clear, decision-ready insights for MPR and key stakeholders.

Cross-functional performance improvement: partner with Sales, Marketing and Operations to improve margin through mix, cost, losses, and portfolio initiatives.

Finance standards and controls: ensure disciplined governance, compliance and adherence to company policies.




What you'll bring

5+ year’s minimum work experience that complements financial training; experience in FCMG  

Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance or other numerical discipline

CA/CPA or equivalent is desirable

Ability to business partner with head of clusters to drive both the long- and short-term delivery of the P&L 

Ability to independently originate ideas and drive to outcomes by coordinating across multiple functions (sales, marketing, revenue management, operations)

Strong analytical skills and financial acumen to assess data and arrive at root cause, with ability to succinctly explain key drivers to senior audience  

Systems knowledge PowerBI, Demantra, JDE, IRI UNIFY highly regarded

Advanced skills in Microsoft Excel and PowerPoint.

Problem-solving mentality with ability to navigate a high pace environment 




Why Join Us?

Be part of a global food company with a rich history and iconic brands

Collaborate with a talented team and contribute to our company's vision

Develop your skills and expertise in a dynamic and supportive environment

Company Description

Bapcor Ltd is the leading automotive aftermarket provider of accessories, equipment and service. We host one of the widest ranges of car parts in the world, with brands including Burson, Autobarn, Midas, Truckline, Premier Auto Trade and Baxters.

We’re more than just automotive — we’re a dynamic, purpose-led organisation powered by people. Our Melbourne-based corporate team plays a critical role in shaping the strategy and success of the Bapcor Group.

In joining our team, you’ll be welcomed into a supportive business that spans more than 1,000 locations with over 5,000 team members across Australia, New Zealand and Thailand. We are passionate about our team, with safety and inclusivity at the forefront of everything we do. We’re also passionate about our customers, products, and living our values of:

Doing the right thing
Being in it together
Giving a damn
Getting it done
Job Description

Bapcor is seeking a Senior Business Analyst to support a broad portfolio of business and technology change initiatives across a complex, multi‑brand retail and distribution environment.

This role is available on a full‑time or part‑time basis for the right candidate and is best suited to a mid‑career Business Analyst who enjoys working through ambiguity, understanding how the business operates end‑to‑end, and translating that into practical, deliverable solutions.

While project experience is important, this is not a purely “project BA” role — it’s hands‑on, process‑driven, and spans discovery through to delivery and BAU transition.

You’ll work across multiple initiatives, partnering closely with business stakeholders, product, and delivery teams to shape outcomes that improve how Bapcor operates at scale.

What You’ll Be Responsible For

Leading requirements elicitation across complex initiatives, including workshops and stakeholder interviews
Analysing and documenting business requirements with clear traceability to outcomes

Optus is a leading Australian telecommunications provider, delivering more than 11 million services every day across mobile, broadband and digital solutions.

We're seeking a Risk Governance and Performance Manager to strengthen risk, controls, and compliance across our Consumer Business Unit. Working at the intersection of data, risk, and decision‑making, you'll translate complex information into clear insights that support leaders, protect customers, and drive effective frontline execution.

With end‑to‑end oversight of Consumer risk and compliance, you'll partner with senior leaders, risk and compliance teams, and analytics to identify trends, surface emerging risks, and support governance forums. This is an ideal role for someone who enjoys working with data, influencing stakeholders, and connecting risk frameworks to real customer, regulatory, and business outcomes.

What You'll Do

Create a consistent approach to governance within the Line 1 Risk & Controls team and play a lead role in preparing materials for Consumer Business unit governance forums and underlying business profile risk forums
Put that approach into practice, collaborating with other members of the line 1 risk team and business partners to assemble governance materials that provide timely and accurate information to enable oversight and decision making.
Design and deliver automated reporting tools and dashboard that provides clear visibility of controls performance, issues, incidents, risks and compliance obligations, enabling day to day management of risk as well as good governance outcomes across Consumer.
Establish and track key risk indicators (KRIs), control metrics, and leading indicators aligned to risk appetite.
Analyse data to identify control gaps, systemic issues, and emerging risks, escalating material findings through governance forums.
Translate complex datasets into clear, compelling insights to support senior leadership decision‑making.
Partner with Line 1, Line 2, analytics, IT, and risk teams to support assurance activities, control uplift initiatives, and reporting automation.
Act as a trusted advisor to Consumer leaders on control effectiveness and risk performance, while championing a strong risk‑based culture.

What makes you perfect

5+ years' experience in governance, audit, controls performance, risk, compliance, assurance, quality, or insights‑led roles—ideally in a regulated, customer‑facing environment.
Demonstrated experience preparing written materials for senior governance forums related to risk and compliance matters
Strong understanding of risk and control frameworks, governance, and regulatory obligations.
Demonstrated experience delivering meaningful reporting, analytics, or assurance outcomes for senior stakeholders.
Strong analytical capability, with the ability to turn data into practical, outcome‑focused insights.
Excellent communication and stakeholder engagement skills, with confidence influencing across multiple functions, and experience in coaching others to improve outcomes.
A pragmatic, customer‑centric approach to risk—focused on improving outcomes, not checklist compliance.

What's in it for you?

Competitive remuneration and employee discounts. Make life easier (and more affordable) with $80 monthly credit and 25% off Optus products and unique shopping discounts with our retail partners.
Flexible working arrangements with opportunities to work three days in the office, two days remote or home.
Vibrant and collaborative office campus that includes cafes, convenience store, chill out zones, GP, post office, gym and on-site childcare centre.
Competitive leave policies, including additional 'Connected' days to focus on culture, family, health, community, or whatever's important to you.
We support growing families with inclusive, carer-neutral paid Parental Leave of up to 16 weeks.
Direct your own development and build future-ready skills with Optus U's industry-focused micro-credentials, developed with La Trobe and Macquarie Universities.
Build meaningful connections through employee-led networks and diversity initiatives including Culture Connect, Elevate Women, Disability Network and Express Yourself (LGBTQIA+).
Put your wellbeing first with free access to counselling and support services, 24/7 — in-person, by phone, SMS, or video.

At Optus, we are strengthened by others and that means valuing diversity and saying ‘yes' to embracing individual differences. We are committed to ensuring that our application process provides an equal employment opportunity to all job seekers, including individuals from diverse gender, cultural and linguistic backgrounds, individuals with a disability, individuals identifying as being part of the LGBTQIA+ community, individuals who may have served in the armed forces or who identify as Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander. We also want to do our best to make our recruitment process inclusive. If you require any adjustments or accessibility support to participate fairly and equitably in the recruitment process, please email AccessibleRecruitment@optus.com.au or call 1800 309 170. We're here to help. When you contact us, please advise your preferred method of contact and we will be in touch within 48 hours.

For more information on Diversity, Inclusion & Belonging at Optus, please visit https://www.optus.com.au/about/inclusion-diversity.

Represent the company as a thought leader in AI — contributing to proposals, events, and market positioning.
Strategy, Thought Leadership & Team Building

Develop and maintain the company's overarching Digital Experience & AI Strategy, keeping it current with market trends and emerging capability.
Build and articulate a compelling vision for how digital and AI capability differentiates our MSP offering.
Mentor and develop talent across the business with an interest in digital and AI disciplines
Experience

10+ years in technology strategy, consulting, or digital transformation roles.
Proven experience delivering AI or automation programs in a business operations context.
Hands-on experience in or leading software / application development — either in-house or in a consulting/agency setting.
Experience engaging C-suite and executive stakeholders, both internally and as clients.
Background in managed services, IT consulting, or professional services highly regarded.
Skills & Capabilities

Strong commercial acumen: able to build business cases, size opportunities, and articulate value.
Exceptional communicator: equally effective in a workshop room, boardroom, or written brief.
Ability to translate complex AI and technology concepts into clear, business-relevant language.
Pragmatic and delivery-focused: comfortable operating without a team or established playbook.
Entrepreneurial mindset: energised by building something new from the ground up.
Knowledge

Deep familiarity with the current AI landscape: LLMs, agentic AI, automation platforms, AI-assisted development tools.
Understanding of MSP business models, service delivery, and the IT landscape of mid-market organisations.
Familiarity with modern software development practices (Agile, DevOps, cloud-native).

About Ubank

At Ubank, we help the digital generation get ahead with money. We’re an everyday money companion, bringing savings, spending, and insights together in one smart, simple experience. From building better savings habits to buying your first home sooner, we’re here to help you move forward with confidence.

The Opportunity This role is for a data professional who loves turning complexity into clarity. You’ll help power data‑driven decisions across Ubank by building reliable pipelines, strong data models, and scalable analytics solutions.

You’re curious, technically hands‑on, and excited by modern platforms and tools. Most importantly, you want to shape how data is used at a fast‑growing, digital‑first bank.

What You’ll Do

Design and build end‑to‑end data pipelines that connect systems and enable trusted insights.
Partner with stakeholders to understand the why, not just the what, behind data needs.
Model and map data flows aligned to our Data Vault architecture.
Embed strong documentation, testing, and quality controls across data assets.
Develop curated datasets and analytics solutions with Analysts and business teams.
Explore and adopt new data sources, platforms, and engineering techniques.
Collaborate with engineers, platform teams, and external partners to deliver at scale.
What You Bring
5+ years’ experience designing, building, and deploying complex data pipelines and datasets.
Strong hands‑on experience with data modelling, transformation, and preparation.
Experience with data streaming technologies (e.g. Kafka, Flink).
Solid knowledge of modern data platforms: data lakes, lakehouse/cloud environments, APIs, and relational databases.
Practical experience with CI/CD and Infrastructure‑as‑Code.
Strong SQL and proficiency in Python, Spark/PySpark, Java, and dbt.
Excellent stakeholder engagement skills and a collaborative mindset.
A natural curiosity and ability to problem‑solve independently.
Nice to Have
Experience with Databricks.
Exposure to Growth, Marketing or Martech data domains.
Knowledge of 3NF data modelling.
Experience with AWS, Airflow, Fivetran, or cloud‑based PostgreSQL.
Why Ubank
Make real impact: Your work will directly influence how ubank grows and serves customers.
Innovate together: A highly collaborative culture where ideas are encouraged and supported.
Build something meaningful: We give you trust, tools, and freedom to create change.
Be yourself: Flexible hybrid working and a culture that values individuality.
Accelerate your career: Clear development paths and opportunities to grow fast.
This is a Sydney‑based permanent role and requires Australian or New Zealand citizenship or permanent residency.

We proudly acknowledge the Traditional Custodians of the lands where we work and live.

We welcome applicants of all backgrounds and can’t wait to connect.

Apply now with your CV and help shape the future of data at Ubank.

Please note, we’re not accepting unsolicited candidate submissions from recruitment agencies. We work with a dedicated panel of partners, so kindly ask agencies not to contact Ubank team members directly.

Job Title: Project Manager / Lead Business Analyst - Fund Accounting
Ad Text:
About the Company
Davidson is partnering with a well-regarded organisation within the superannuation sector to appoint an experienced Project Manager / Lead Business Analyst on a 12-month fixed term contract. This is an exciting opportunity to join a business delivering important change across investment operations, finance and technology, with a strong focus on fund accounting and unit pricing environments.

About the Role
The Project Manager / Lead Business Analyst will play a pivotal role in leading and shaping complex initiatives across fund accounting, unit pricing and broader investment operations. Sitting at the intersection of business, finance and technology, this role will be responsible for driving delivery outcomes while also leading business analysis activities to ensure requirements are clearly defined, agreed and translated into effective solutions.

This role will suit someone who can move comfortably between strategic stakeholder engagement, detailed business analysis and end-to-end project delivery.

Key responsibilities will include:

Leading end-to-end delivery of initiatives across fund accounting, unit pricing and investment operations
Partnering with business, finance and technology stakeholders to identify needs, define requirements and deliver fit-for-purpose solutions
Leading business analysis activities including requirement gathering, process mapping, impact assessments and documentation
Managing project plans, governance, risks, issues, dependencies and reporting across multiple workstreams
Translating business requirements into clear delivery plans and actionable outcomes in collaboration with technical teams
Driving workshops and stakeholder discussions to build alignment, clarify priorities and support decision-making
Supporting system and process improvements across investment and funds management operations
Coordinating internal teams and external vendors to ensure successful delivery and implementation outcomes
Acting as the key link between business stakeholders and delivery teams to ensure solutions align with operational and strategic objectives

About You
The ideal candidate will bring a blend of project management and lead business analysis capability, with a strong track record of delivering complex change within superannuation, investment operations or broader financial services environments.

You will bring:

Proven experience in a Project Manager, Lead Business Analyst or hybrid delivery role within financial services
Strong experience working across fund accounting and unit pricing environments
Deep understanding of superannuation, investments or funds management operations
Demonstrated ability to lead both project delivery and business analysis activities across finance and technology functions
Strong capability in requirements gathering, process analysis, workshop facilitation and stakeholder engagement
Experience working on system-led change initiatives in partnership with technology teams
Strong governance, planning, risk and issue management capability
Experience engaging with external vendors, implementation partners or service providers
Excellent communication, negotiation and relationship management skills
The ability to operate confidently in a fast-paced, evolving and highly regulated environment

The Benefits

Opportunity to contribute to a high-profile multi-year program
12-month fixed term contract with meaningful, business-critical project scope
Hybrid working arrangements with 2 days per week in the office or 8 days per month
Flexibility to be based in either Sydney or Melbourne


Please apply with current resume in Microsoft Word format only (.doc or .docx). If you have the relevant experience above and require further information, please contact Tara Gearon at tara.gearon@davidsonwp.com quoting reference JN -042026-42915. Want to know more about Davidson? Visit us at www.davidsonwp.com

The Company
Our client is a large ASX‑listed organisation undergoing a significant period of growth, portfolio optimisation and strategic asset activity. With a strong pipeline of acquisitions, divestments and major capital projects, they are seeking a commercially minded Finance Analyst to join their high‑performing corporate finance team. This role is perfect for someone who thrives in a fast‑paced, deal‑driven environment and brings experience from projects, engineering, construction, infrastructure or other asset‑intensive industries.

The Role
Reporting to the CFO and working close with the Corporate Finance Manager, you will play a key role in supporting the organisation’s M&A activity, asset transactions and strategic investment decisions. Your work will directly influence executive‑level decision making and long‑term portfolio strategy. Some of your duties will include:

Financial analysis and modelling for acquisitions, divestments and capital projects
Supporting due diligence across financial, operational and commercial workstreams
Preparing valuation models, scenario analysis and investment papers
Supporting the month‑end and reporting process for M&A‑related accounting impacts
About You
You will be an experienced Finance Analyst who has worked in due diligence and M&A environments who is able to independently work on analysis of financials and also create scenario based models. Ideally you would have started your career in Big 4 or in a M&A consulting environment.

To Apply
If you are interested in discussing please apply through this advert or email Simon Clancy at sclancy@fourquarters.com.au

Ad Text:
Passionate about education?

Want to help improve outcomes for schools and students?

Reviews and School Improvement (RSI) provides system monitoring and assurance to support continuous improvement in Queensland state schools and education centres by providing leadership and oversight for targeted insights reviews. In addition, the RSI contributes significantly to distilling and sharing evidence-based insights and effective practices, to inform school improvement across the Queensland education system. Insights reviews (behaviour) will provide schools with practical guidance to strengthen their discipline systems and practices, enabling every school to deliver excellence through maintaining high expectations, a positive learning culture and quality teaching and learning.

The successful candidate will be employed at the classification listed on the attached role description under EOSD provisions.

About the Role

RSI are seeking Principals, Insights Reviews (behaviour) with particular knowledge and experience in leading effective and responsive school systems and practices which support student engagement for learning and providing effective support and interventions for student with diverse learning needs and complex behaviours.

As the Principal, Insights Reviews you will:

Lead insights reviews (behaviour) across Queensland State Schools to inform school and system improvement.
Play a key role in the delivery of the insights reviews as a team member.
Contribute to development of insights reviews methodology and processes.

Responsibilities include:

Deliver insights reviews, aligned to system priorities and initiatives, using evidence-based tools and methodologies.
Conduct insights reviews in line with agreed methodologies and processes.
As chair, lead teams, including the support and mentoring of review team members.
Capture high-quality evidence in reports that generate insights to inform school, region and system decision making.
Identify and share effective practice.
Collaboratively develop and implement approaches responsive to current and emerging system priorities.
Foster high quality collaboration, communication, cultural awareness and information sharing to enable an understanding of the complexities and challenges that exist in specific targeted areas.
Develop a rapport and engage with a range of external and internal stakeholders including Principals, school leaders, teachers, school staff, students, parents, regional staff and carers and community members to effectively and collaboratively enable an integrated approach to deliver the vision of the department.
Provide advice and expertise to improve school systems and processes that promote engagement and educational outcomes for all students, including those with diverse and/or complex learning needs.
Understand and apply the legislation and policies that influence schooling especially in relation to students with diverse and/or complex learning needs and young people who are not actively engaged in a learning pathway.
Develop, articulate and promote a shared educational vision focused on providing quality learning outcomes for all students, including those with diverse and/or complex learning needs.

The timing and location of school reviews can be a determining factor in when the employee is appointed.

Successful applicants will be expected to attend training prior to undertaking the role.

Applicants must be available to travel, including overnight absences.

Mandatory Requirements:

You must be a permanent employee of the department with a substantive teaching or classified officer role employed under Teaching in State Education Award - State 2016, have current full registration as a teacher and should be aware that temporary internal moves for the purposes of the department's payroll processes are referred to as internal secondments
You must be currently employed as a Principal or Acting Principal in a Queensland state school
Employees engaged temporarily as Education Officer - Special Duties have a requirement of 36¼ hours per week attendance and arrangements as outlined in clause 7.4 of the Department of Education State School Teachers Certified Agreement 2019

Interested? To Apply: 

Attach a brief resume (maximum 2 pages) including contact details for 2 referees (referees should have an understanding of your relevant work history)
Attach a maximum two-page response demonstrating your experiences in leading a school improvement agenda, utilising strong interpersonal skills to be an effective leader, and your suitability for the role
Completion of the Principal, Insights Reviews questionnaire (Please note: once you have submitted your application documents and pressed next, you will be prompted to answer the required questionnaire)

We welcome all applicants to share any support needed to ensure our recruitment process is inclusive.

Applications remain current for 12 months from the closing date and may be considered for appointment to identical or similar vacancies within the Department.

Applications from recruitment agencies will not be accepted.

Your new opportunity
Queensland Treasury plays a central role in supporting the Government's infrastructure and investment priorities. We are seeking a Principal Commercial Analyst to join our Commercial Project Advisory team within the Infrastructure Projects group.

In this role, you will work on complex and high-profile commercial matters, supporting government engagement with major infrastructure projects and private sector partners. You will contribute to practical, well-considered advice that informs investment decisions and delivery approaches across government, helping ensure projects are affordable, viable and deliver long-term value for Queenslanders.

This is an opportunity to apply your commercial expertise in a trusted central agency environment, where your work will directly influence outcomes of state-wide significance.

In this role, you will:
• Support the delivery of high-quality commercial, financial and transaction advice to government agencies and Treasury, particularly in relation to investment decisions, business cases, procurement and delivery models.
• Contribute to the development and assessment of delivery strategies, including Public Private Partnerships and other commercial models, by analysing risks, market capability and commercial structures.
• Analyse financial and commercial documentation such as feasibility assessments, financial models, cost estimates and demand forecasts to support robust, evidence-based recommendations.
• Support commercial assurance activities by constructively challenging assumptions, methodologies and commercial positions to strengthen project proposals.
• Build and manage effective working relationships with stakeholders across government and industry to support market engagement, negotiations and coordinated advice.
• Assist in resolving complex issues by contributing to timely, practical and outcome-focused solutions, including leading or supporting responses when issues arise.
• Build strong networks across government and industry to support successful project outcomes.

What experience you will bring:
• Demonstrated commercial experience in infrastructure, investment or major project environments, with a strong focus on value for money, affordability and appropriate risk allocation.
• Strong commercial acumen within a government, regulatory or policy context.
• Highly developed stakeholder engagement and relationship management skills, with the ability to work effectively across government, central agencies and industry to support coordinated, outcome-focused advice.
• The ability to apply commercial judgement in a government setting, synthesising financial, economic and strategic inputs to support sound investment decisions.

Whilst there are no mandatory qualifications needed for this role, relevant qualifications will be highly regarded.

How to apply
To submit your application, click on the Apply link to provide:
• Your resume detailing relevant experience
• A cover letter of no more than two pages outlining why you are interested in this position and how your knowledge, skills and experience makes you a strong candidate for the role.

Queensland Treasury is committed to building an inclusive and diverse workforce that reflects the community we serve and treating all applicants equitably. This starts with recruitment, including making reasonable adjustments to support applicants at all stages of the process.
